source_note: Ingested source is the BlackRock Equity Market Outlook (snapshot labelled 'Q3 2026' / Lit No. EQ-OUTLOOK-3Q-2026; task header says Q2 2026 — immaterial label drift). It is an equity-ONLY Fundamental/Systematic outlook. The GT commentary column repeatedly references BII Global Outlook, MyMap multi-asset dials, and Weekly Commentary covering bonds/credit/rates — none of which exist in this single equity PDF. That, plus a visible leaf<->commentary offset in the worksheet, indicates the GT was authored from a fuller BlackRock multi-asset corpus. Classifications below judge each GT leaf+view strictly against THIS ingested equity source.

rows.note: The source's EM enthusiasm is explicitly Asia-centric (Taiwan, South Korea, semis). Brazil is never named; 'Latin America' appears only in distribution disclaimers. The Brazil O comes from the fuller BII/multi-asset corpus, not this equity outlook.
No defence/aerospace content anywhere in the ingested source; the GT commentary about European defence stocks (+350%, NATO) is from other BlackRock material.

Only a passing positive: utilities 'pay a steady dividend... potential to deliver a higher yield than the broader equity markets.' That is a supporting characteristic of the infrastructure/utilities thesis, not a standalone income/dividend-factor call — too weak to require its own row.

Source states a 'sustained preference for the physical infrastructure layer of AI over more asset-light technology businesses facing disruption concerns' plus mega-cap AI FCF drawing 'toward zero' and lowest S&P FCF yield in 25 years — a clear relative-underweight of expensive/asset-light tech. The model captured NO cautionary tech call; it took the opposite side via Technology (Sector) O on Asian semis. Same underlying theme as the US Mega-Cap U gap below.
No Latin America equity call in the source; EM positivity is Asia-specific. LatAm appears only in disclaimers.

The Systematic section discusses price momentum's exceptional strength and fundamental momentum's weakness descriptively and pitches going 'upstream' beyond crowded factors, but assigns no explicit OW/N/UW momentum call — a Neutral is a reasonable but non-required inference.

Quality is touched only obliquely ('signals related to profitability, accounting trends' within fundamental momentum). No Quality-factor stance is stated; the Neutral call originates outside this source.
The source is optimistic on European banks but makes no UK large-cap call. GT commentary (MyMap/Pacific ex-Japan) is from other material.
No UK small-cap content in the ingested source; the UK-small-cap-vs-Mexico-discount thesis is from the fuller corpus.

The document's core pitch — diversify away from narrow mega-cap concentration into 'a larger cross-section' and 'frostier corners' — is the equal-weight/breadth thesis. The model expressed this via Global Equities O plus a wide fan of sector calls (energy/materials/healthcare/infra), so the view is covered on nearby leaves even though no equal-weight leaf was emitted.

Caution on expensive US large/mega-cap is present (lowest FCF yield in 25 years; vulnerable to profit taking) but no explicit US-large-cap Neutral call; it is a milder derivative of the mega-cap caution captured more sharply below.

The source's central counterweight: mega-cap AI winners are 'particularly vulnerable to profit taking,' their FCF is 'draw(n) down toward zero,' some 'issuing debt,' and S&P 500 FCF yield is the lowest in 25 years — the whole thesis is diversifying AWAY from them. The model emitted zero calls capturing this; it only made positive tech calls. A genuine, costly miss (U vs N arguable, but the caution is unambiguous).

The FCF-diversification thesis (buying cheap energy/materials/healthcare away from expensive growth) is value-flavoured, but no explicit value-factor call is stated; the model expressed the same idea through cheap-sector O calls.

Source is clearly constructive on healthcare: historically high FCF margins in pharma and health equipment, health equipment's 5% FCF yield and low AI correlation make it 'a potential diversifier,' valuations 'compelling' at a 16% discount vs a 20% historical premium. Model's O is grounded; GT's N is too conservative. Model was review-flagged only due to scrambled two-column text, but the snapshot text confirms the quote.
'Overall, we begin the second half of 2026 with a constructive outlook for equities.' Directly grounded.
Energy FCF yield 8% 'well above tech and the long-term average,' higher-for-longer oil prices, 'investment case for the sector may be more durable than the market expects.' Well grounded.
Materials cycle 'long-duration and well-heeled,' metals & mining FCF yields over 7%, reshoring/short-supply tailwind. Grounded.
'large parts of the listed infrastructure universe are set to benefit,' listed infra at a discount to global equities, bottlenecks 'provide opportunities.' Grounded.
'bright outlook for emerging market stocks, particularly in Asia'; EM earnings forecast to grow over 35% this year, more than any region. Directly grounded.
EM strength concentrated in 'the northern part of Asia'; Asian semiconductor makers as primary AI-spend beneficiaries. Grounded.
'strongest opportunities have emerged through more discrete expressions within the infrastructure, power and industrial layers'; grid-equipment providers (turbines, cables, transformers). Grounded.

'longer-term supply shock, resulting in structurally tighter supply and higher prices'; higher-for-longer oil prices. Grounded, though derived from the energy-equity thesis rather than a pure commodity call.

Metals & mining FCF yields over 7% inside an explicitly 'long-duration and well-heeled' materials cycle with reshoring tailwinds — the positive stance is contextually clear, not a bare stat as the checker feared.
'clean energy stocks and energy efficiency names are also on lower valuations than the broader market' and named as direct beneficiaries; 'these bottlenecks provide opportunities.' Grounded.

Inferred country call, verbatim support present: 'earnings in Taiwan forecast to grow 34% in 2026' within the bright Asia/semis outlook. Grounded and recall-positive; correctly segregated one band below stated calls with review flag.

Inferred country call, strong verbatim support: SK earnings forecast +220%, government corporate-reform program 'with the aim of increasing shareholder value,' shipbuilders benefiting from geopolitical fragmentation. Grounded and recall-positive.

Inferred call, verbatim support present: AI investment 'flowing to the large Asian makers of the semiconductors,' memory demand '20%-30% above supply.' Grounded FOR the Asian-semi segment — but note this is the model's tech call and it is one-sided: the source is two-sided on tech and GT captured the offsetting caution (IT/Tech U, Mega-Cap U) that the model omitted.

summary.country_granularity_note: Taiwan Equities O and South Korea Equities O (inferred) are both grounded and recall-POSITIVE. The source names each country with concrete data — Taiwan earnings +34% (L362-363), South Korea earnings +220% plus a corporate-reform/shareholder-value program and shipbuilder tailwinds (L363-372, L401-404) — inside the bullish Asia/semis thesis. GT did not enumerate country leaves, so these inferences add recall the GT lacks. Correctly segregated one band below stated calls with review flags.
summary.inferred_tier_note: All three inferred-basis rows (Taiwan, South Korea, Technology Sector) have verbatim supporting spans in the ingested source (earnings figures, corporate-reform language, memory-shortage stats). None hallucinated; each is appropriately review-flagged and confidence-capped. Inferred tier is sound.
summary.headline: True recall on THIS equity-only source is strong: all 13 model calls are grounded (13/13 sound_breadth, zero overreach/hallucination), the lone view disagreement resolves model_correct (Healthcare O is squarely supported at L223-234), and the inferred Taiwan/South Korea country calls are grounded and add recall GT never enumerated. The dominant miss cause is not model failure but GT provenance: 10 of 13 misses (6 not_grounded + 4 defensible_omission) reference bonds, credit, and MyMap/BII multi-asset dials absent from this single equity PDF, so the GT was clearly authored from a fuller BlackRock corpus. The one real, costly defect is a genuine recall_gap on the document's CENTRAL cautionary stance: the source repeatedly warns that expensive US mega-cap/asset-light AI winners are 'vulnerable to profit taking' (FCF toward zero, lowest S&P FCF yield in 25 years) and pitches diversifying AWAY from them (GT: US Mega-Cap U + IT/Tech U), yet the model emitted only bullish tech (Technology Sector O on Asian semis), taking the opposite side and never flagging the caution.
